back_translation: Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood — two and a half cubits its length, a cubit and a half its width, and a cubit and a half its height.
He overlaid it with pure gold, inside and out, and made a gold molding around it.
He cast for it four gold rings on its four feet — two rings on one side and two rings on the other side.
He made poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
He inserted the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark, to carry the ark.
He made a mercy seat of pure gold, two and a half cubits its length, and a cubit and a half its width.
He made two cherubim of gold, hammered work he made them, from the two ends of the mercy seat.
One cherub from one end and one cherub from the other end; from the mercy seat he made the cherubim from its two ends.
The cherubim had wings spread upward, covering the mercy seat with their wings; and their faces, one toward another, were toward the mercy seat.
He made the table of acacia wood, two cubits its length, a cubit its width, and a cubit and a half its height.
He overlaid it with pure gold and made a gold molding around it.
He made a rim a handbreadth wide around it, and made a gold molding for its rim around it.
He cast for it four gold rings and set the rings on the four corners that are at its four legs.
Close to the rim were the rings — holders for the poles to carry the table.
He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold, to carry the table.
He made the vessels that were on the table — its plates, its dishes, its bowls, and its pitchers from which to pour out — of pure gold.
He made the lampstand of pure gold; hammered work he made the lampstand. Its base and its shaft, its cups, its buds, and its petals were of one piece with it.
Six branches extended from its sides — three branches of the lampstand from its one side, and three branches of the lampstand from its other side.

Three cups shaped like almond blossoms on the one branch — bud and petal — and three cups shaped like almond blossoms on one branch — bud and petal — so for the six branches extending from the lampstand.
And on the lampstand itself were four cups shaped like almond blossoms — its buds and its petals.
A bud under the first pair of branches from it, and a bud under the second pair of branches from it, and a bud under the third pair of branches from it — for the six branches extending from it.
Their buds and their branches were of one piece with it — all of it one hammered work of pure gold.
He made its seven lamps, its wick trimmers, and its trays of pure gold.
He made it of a talent of pure gold, with all its vessels.
He made the altar of incense of acacia wood; a cubit its length, a cubit its width — square — and two cubits its height. Its horns were of one piece with it.
He overlaid it with pure gold — its top and its sides round about and its horns — and made a gold molding around it.
He made two gold rings for it under its molding, on its two sides, on its two opposite sides — as holders for poles to carry it with them.
He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
He made the sacred anointing oil and the pure fragrant incense — the work of a perfumer.
